answer:
**No verbatim use of the Sanskrit term *avidyā* (or *ajñāna*) appears in the Old Javanese *tattva* texts of the Falsafa corpus (namely *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, *San Hyan Mahajnana*, and *Gaṇapatitattva*).**[^1]

Instead, the doctrinal concept of ignorance—unconsciousness, delusion (*moha*), forgetfulness (*lupa*), and absence of discriminative knowledge (*jñāna*)—is rendered through the tattva-scheme of *acetana* ("unconsciousness") and *māyātattva* (the "unconscious principle," a child of *māyā/prakṛti* and source of *triguṇa*, *citta*, bondage, and saṃsāra).[^2] These texts follow a 36-tattva Śaiva framework (Śivatattva/*cetana* vs. Māyātattva/*acetana*), where ignorance is not a standalone term but the defining quality of Māyātattva: "forgetfulness, delusion, the absence of teachings... no *cetana*, no *jñāna*; it is merely forgetfulness... a vast and empty void, without foundation, forgetful, contrary."[^3] *Prakṛti*/*māyā* is the "night" or "wife/mother," binding *puruṣa* (the "sun"/"father") in saṃsāra.[^4]

This substitution (Sanskrit *acetana*/*māyā*-tattva over *avidyā*) signals **deference to authoritative Sanskritic Āgama/Tantra sources** (e.g., *Mālinīvijayottara*, *Śivadharma*, or Kashmir Śaiva *tattva*-lists), adapted into Kawi prose for Javanese yogic/esoteric instruction. Retaining *tattva*-jargon preserves pan-Indian Śaiva orthodoxy (*māyā* as limiting potency below pure Śiva), while *lupa*/*acetana* localizes it as "forgetfulness" (evoking Advaita-*avidyā* but Śaiva-framed). No "demotic" Javanese neologism fully replaces it; authority flows from Sanskrit roots, not innovation.[^5]

[^1]: Confirmed via `search_corpus("avidya")`, `search_corpus("ajñāna OR ajnana")`, `search_corpus("ignorance")`, and full reads of *San Hyan Tattvajnana* [ch.1](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/), *San Hyan Mahajnana* [ch.1](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/), *Gaṇapatitattva* [ch.1](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/).

[^2]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch.1 [p-daaeef](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-daaeef).

[^3]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, ch.1 [p-daaeef](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-daaeef); cf. *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch.1 [p-5c91e6](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-5c91e6) (*prakṛti* as "night").

[^4]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch.1 [p-1ce441](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-1ce441), [p-c60edd](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-c60edd).

[^5]: *Gaṇapatitattva* lacks *avidyā* but echoes *māyā*-illusion (*p-54* ff.); corpus has no other Old Javanese *tattva* texts.
